research.name: Radiative Transfer in Protoplanetary Discs
research.summary: Master's thesis and extended research project. Paper in preparation.
research.abstract: Over the past decade, SPHERE scattered light observations of protoplanetary discs have revealed previously unseen features with unprecedented resolution. One such feature are radial streaks of reduced brightness that are commonly interpreted as shadows. A potential cause for these shadows is an embedded companion within the disc. In this work, we use 3D radiative transfer simulations with RADMC-3D to investigate the shadowing effects of embedded companions across a range of orbital distances (1-30 au) and companion masses (0.5-30 M_J). We model 0.1 µm dust grains, which are well-coupled to the gas disc, to produce scattered light images. The images are then convolved to typical observational resolutions. By normalising the total image to the azimuthal mean brightness, we are able to identify trends across the parameter space. Our results provide empirical solutions for the width and depth of shadows as a function of companion mass and location.
research.supervisors: Dr Giulia Ballabio and Dr Anna Penzlin

research.name: Astronomical Image Processing
research.summary: Computational physics project as part of third year laboratory at Imperial.
research.abstract: This report presents a galaxy survey from a deep sky CCD image taken at the Mayall 4-metre Telescope, analysed using object detection and identification algorithms. The study aimed to determine the galaxy number counts for an optical filter with a central wavelength of 625nm, and to compare the data with theory. A total of 3091 ± 20 galaxies were catalogued, with calibrated magnitudes ranging between 9.6 and 18.8. The logarithm of the number count was plotted as a function of magnitude, and a linear regression of gradient 0.35 was observed for a magnitude range of 10 to 17.5.

research.name: Analysis of the B^0 → K^{*0} µ⁻ µ⁺ Decay
research.summary: Worked in a 20-person team to analyse LHCb data, with the goal of reconstructing the rare B^0 decay from a large sample of beauty meson decays. The angular distribution was compared with the predictions of the Standard Model. Multivariate selections and manual cuts were done to separate the candidate data.

research.name: Coronal Heating: Modelling the Sun's Magnetic Field
research.summary: Generated a Potential Field Source Surface (PFSS) model of the Sun's magnetic field. Investigated the evolution of the magnetic field through a full solar cycle, using data obtained from the National Solar Observatory GONG Magnetograms. An extension of this project would be to compare actual magnetic field measurements to the predictions obtained by the PFSS model via ballistic propagation.
